html:
Exclusive to:

Tropical Fish is a type of Fish Food introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC.

They can be obtained by fishing in a Shoal or Tidal Pool with a Fishing Rod or rowing over it with Boats with an attached Trawl Net. Fish Farms that have been stocked with Roe have a chance to continually produce Tropical Fish.

Merms and Fishermerms will drop Tropical Fish when killed. Hammering down Merm Huts and Fishermerm's Huts will also yield Tropical Fish, however these structures are not renewable and destroying them will also remove the source of Merm and Fishermerms respectively. Fishermerms will fish in Tidal Pools on their own for Tropical Fish, but they will not eat what they catch, which then can then be collected by the player. Except for Fish Farms and Shoals, which are commonly found in Coral Reef and Mangrove biomes, all other sources of Tropical Fish can only be found in Tidal Marsh biomes.

Tropical Fish can be used in Crock Pot, and counts as 0.5 units Meats and 1 units of Fishes. Cooking Tropical Fish on a Fire will yield a Cooked Fish Morsel. It can also be dried to get Small Jerky. It can be traded to Yaarctopus for Dubloons.

Tips

  • Tropical Fish has more meat and fish value than Cooked Fish Morsel obtained by cooking it on fire, so it is not recommended to cook Tropical Fishes if they are going to be used in the Crock Pot. Drying them also has a similar effect, but it may be more desirable since Small Jerky can be used in recipes that Tropical Fish cannot be, due to many Fish based recipes taking precedence over Meat based ones.
  • Fish Farms that are stocking Tropical Fish are marked with a small sign showing a blue fish, after being harvested at least once.

Trivia

  • Tropical Fish resemble the real-life angelfish.
  • Tropical Fish originally had its own cooked version.

Gallery

  • A Fish Farm stocking Tropical Fish, visible from the blue fish on the small sign.
  • Unused Cooked Tropical Fish
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

They can be obtained by fishing in a Shoal or Tidal Pool with a Fishing Rod or rowing over it with Boats with an attached Trawl Net. Fish Farms that have been stocked with Roe have a chance to continually produce Tropical Fish.

Fishermerms will fish in Tidal Pools on their own for Tropical Fish, but they will not eat what they catch, which then can then be collected by the player. Except for Fish Farms and Shoals, which are commonly found in Coral Reef and Mangrove biomes, all other sources of Tropical Fish can only be found in Tidal Marsh biomes.

Tropical Fish has more meat and fish value than Cooked Fish Morsel obtained by cooking it on fire, so it is not recommended to cook Tropical Fishes if they are going to be used in the Crock Pot. Drying them also has a similar effect, but it may be more desirable since Small Jerky can be used in recipes that Tropical Fish cannot be, due to many Fish based recipes taking precedence over Meat based ones.

But I dare not consume it raw. |wigfrid = Inside it is the cölör öf meat! |webber = It's pretty! |walani = Such tasty colors! |warly = Catch of today! |woodlegs = Aye, me favorite food! |wilba = LOOKETH LIKE 'TIS NUMMYS |wormwood = Pretty Glub Glub |wheeler = Looks like it'd make a beautiful lunch. |choose = warly}} '''Tropical Fish''' is a type of [[Fishes|Fish]] [[Food]] introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. They can be obtained by fishing in a [[Shoal]] or [[Tidal Pool]] with a [[Fishing Rod]] or rowing over it with [[Boats]] with an attached [[Trawl Net]]. [[Fish Farm]]s that have been stocked with [[Roe]] have a chance to continually produce Tropical Fish. [[Merm]]s and [[Fishermerm]]s will drop Tropical Fish when killed. [[Hammer]]ing down [[Merm Hut]]s and [[Fishermerm's Hut]]s will also yield Tropical Fish, however these structures are not renewable and destroying them will also remove the source of Merm and Fishermerms respectively. Fishermerms will fish in Tidal Pools on their own for Tropical Fish, but they will not eat what they catch, which then can then be collected by the player. Except for Fish Farms and Shoals, which are commonly found in [[Coral Reef]] and [[Mangrove]] biomes, all other sources of Tropical Fish can only be found in [[Tidal Marsh]] biomes. Tropical Fish can be used in [[Crock Pot]], and counts as 0.5 units [[Meats]] and 1 units of [[Fishes]]. Cooking Tropical Fish on a [[Fire]] will yield a [[Fish Morsel|Cooked Fish Morsel]]. It can also be [[Drying Rack|dried]] to get [[Small Jerky]]. It can be traded to [[Yaarctopus]] for [[Dubloons]]. __NOTOC__ =={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe|item=Tropical Fish|result=Cooked Fish Morsel|tool = Cooking}} {{Recipe|item1=Tropical Fish|result=Small Jerky|tool = Drying Rack}} {{Recipe|item=Tropical Fish|tool = Birdcage|result=Egg}} {{Recipe|item=Tropical Fish|result=Octopus Chest|tool = Yaarctopus Icon}} =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * Tropical Fish has more meat and fish value than Cooked Fish Morsel obtained by cooking it on fire, so it is not recommended to cook Tropical Fishes if they are going to be used in the Crock Pot. Drying them also has a similar effect, but it may be more desirable since Small Jerky can be used in recipes that Tropical Fish cannot be, due to many Fish based recipes taking precedence over Meat based ones. * Fish Farms that are stocking Tropical Fish are marked with a small sign showing a blue fish, after being harvested at least once. == {{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Tropical Fish resemble the real-life angelfish. * Tropical Fish originally had its own [[Unimplemented_Items#Tropical Fish|cooked version]]. =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Fish Farm Tropical Fish.png|A Fish Farm stocking Tropical Fish, visible from the blue fish on the small sign.
